races.name: Itzulia Basque Country – Stage 2
races.description: Stage 2 of the 65th Itzulia Basque Country, the quintessential punchy climbers' stage race. The day is dominated by the Cat.1 San Miguel de Aralar (9.4–9.5km @ 7.7–7.9%), whose summit arrives 19.5km from the finish. After cresting Aralar, riders descend and then face a rolling 3.5km finishing climb to the Cuevas de Mendukilo that reaches close to 10% near the top. Total elevation: 3,268–3,300m over 164.1km. Race organisers describe this as a breakaway stage, but the finale is technical enough for GC differences. Del Toro and Ayuso, who both haemorrhaged big time in yesterday's ITT, are under pressure to attack and reduce the gap to leader Paul Seixas.

broadcasts.notes: Free-to-air on EITB (Basque public broadcaster) in Spanish Basque Country. From race start.
TNT Sports TV and HBO Max streaming from 14:30 BST (13:30 UTC). Coverage runs ~2 hours covering the decisive final phase. Plans from £25.99/month.
Coverage from 9:30 AM EDT (13:30 UTC). Monthly $39.99 / annual $155.88.
Free live and on-demand on SBS.
Eurosport and HBO Max / Discovery+ across Europe, varies by country.

data_confidence.notes: Distance 164.1km confirmed by multiple sources (Cyclingstage, Domestique, CyclingUpToDate). Elevation 3,268–3,300m (minor variance across sources). Stage start 12:20 CEST / 10:20 UTC per CyclingUpToDate stage 2 preview ('estimated to start and finish at 12:20 and 16:30 CET'). Finish ~16:30 CEST / 14:30 UTC. GC standings from Cyclingnews stage 1 report: Seixas wins by 24s over Vauquelin, Großschartner 3rd +27s, Roglič 4th +28s, del Toro ~+51s, Ayuso +1:16 (38th). UK broadcast from 14:30 BST confirmed by ProCyclingUK. US from 9:30 EDT confirmed by HBO Max listing. Race is 65th edition, classified as 2.UWT (15th race of 2026 WT). 22 teams: 16 WT + 6 ProTeams.

races.name: Région Pays de la Loire Tour – Stage 1
races.stage_race_name: Région Pays de la Loire Tour 2026
races.finish_type: Uphill finish (urban circuit, sinuous, multiple passes)
races.description: Stage 1 of the 4th edition of the Région Pays de la Loire Tour — the race's first-ever year as a UCI ProSeries event (previously 2.1 Europe Tour). The grande départ is from the Royal Abbey of Fontevraud in Maine-et-Loire. The 187.5km route crosses through the valleys of the Layon, Aubance and Mauges rivers before arriving in the Loire-Atlantique for the finish in Vertou. Though predominantly rolling rather than mountainous, the stage features short punchy climbs including the Côte du Pont Barré (400m @ 4.5%) and the Côte de la Rouillère (900m @ 6.9%), followed by a sinuous urban final circuit through Vertou run three times. Suits punchy sprinters who can handle the late climbs. 19 teams: includes WorldTeams NSN, Decathlon CMA CGM, Cofidis and others. GC winner will earn 250 UCI points (up from 200 as 2.1). Defending overall champion: Kévin Vauquelin (Arkéa-B&B Hotels, 2025).

broadcasts.notes: All four stages broadcast live on L'Équipe 21 (free-to-air in France). Exact start time of coverage not confirmed; typically begins with ~2hrs to go.

data_confidence.notes: Race dates confirmed: April 7–10 by official site and DirectVelo. Stage 1: Fontevraud-l'Abbaye → Vertou 187.5km confirmed by Cyclismactu, TotalVelo, DirectVelo. 4th edition overall, first year as 2.Pro (UCI ProSeries) confirmed by Wikipedia and official race site. Defending champion Kévin Vauquelin (won 2025 edition as 2.1). Broadcast: L'Équipe 21 for all stages confirmed by Velo101. No international broadcaster identified (ProSeries race, limited international rights). Start time estimated as ~12:00 CEST based on similar 2.Pro French races — not officially confirmed; TotalVelo did not state an exact start time for stage 1. Stage profiles from TotalVelo and DirectVelo.

recent_results.notes: Pogačar attacked on the final Oude Kwaremont with 18km to go to solo in and tie the record as an eight-time three-winner (equalling Van der Poel, Boonen, Cancellara etc). Evenepoel's Monument debut ended with a stunning 3rd. Van Aert 4th. Mads Pedersen noted in top 5. Pogačar's 12th Monument, now second all-time behind Merckx's 19. If he wins Paris-Roubaix next Sunday he'll become the 4th man to win all 5 Monuments. A level crossing closure briefly split the peloton and neutralised part of the race early on.

Vollering attacked on the Oude Kwaremont with ~20km to go, rode solo to a 42-second win — her first Flanders and third Monument career victory. Defending champion Kopecky 4th. Reusser and Kim Le Court-Pienaar both suffered fractures in a crash during the race.

recent_results.race: Itzulia Basque Country Stage 1 – ITT, Bilbao (2.UWT)
recent_results.notes: Seixas claimed his first-ever WorldTour stage win, dominating on the 13.9km punchy ITT (+240m elev) in Bilbao. Del Toro lost 51 seconds; Ayuso had a disastrous day losing 1:16 after a navigational error. Roglič's legendary Itzulia ITT winning record (5 from 5 previously) finally ended.
Navarra classic. Izagirre in fine form ahead of Itzulia.

upcoming.notes: Basauri circuit, 152.8km, 2,900m. Classified as 'could suit breakaway'. Finishes uphill in industrial Basauri.
Men's and women's ProSeries one-day sprint classic in Schoten. Men: 205.2km (Terneuzen → Schoten). Women: 130.3km. Traditional fast sprint finish.

The Hell of the North. 258.3km, Compiègne → Roubaix. Pogačar targeting the Grand Slam of all 5 Monuments. Evenepoel confirmed out (from Flanders crash/fatigue). Reusser and Le Court-Pienaar out due to Tour of Flanders fractures.

notes: Tadej Pogačar won Tour of Flanders on Sunday (3rd win), now targeting Paris-Roubaix next Sunday as potential 5th Monument and grand Classics Slam.
Remco Evenepoel confirmed NOT starting Paris-Roubaix after Flanders effort.
Marlen Reusser and Kim Le Court-Pienaar both confirmed fractured bones from Tour of Flanders crash; out of Paris-Roubaix Femmes.
Itzulia Basque Country 2026 is conspicuously Pogačar-free and Vingegaard-free — pure GC battle among the next generation.
Paul Seixas, 19, is the GC leader at Itzulia after a dominant Stage 1 ITT win. He is now the biggest story in cycling after Pogačar.
No women's WT or ProSeries race today. The Région Pays de la Loire Tour Féminin (1.1 WE) also starts today but is below the WT/Pro threshold requested.

races_excluded.name: Région Pays de la Loire Tour Féminin (Stage 1)
races_excluded.reason: Below WT/Pro threshold requested. Women's 1.1 one-day race running April 7–8 in France.

issues: Itzulia Stage 2 exact start time: Officially listed as ~12:20 CEST (10:20 UTC) per CyclingUpToDate stage 2 preview; cross-referenced with UK TV coverage starting 14:30 BST which aligns with ~2hr broadcast window for a 4hr stage. Used 12:20 CEST.
Région Pays de la Loire Tour Stage 1 start time not officially confirmed in any searched source. Listed as ~12:00 CEST estimated based on comparable 2.Pro French stage races.
Région Pays de la Loire Tour international broadcast: None identified. ProSeries races at this tier have limited rights outside France; only L'Équipe 21 confirmed.
GC gap for Roglič confirmed as +0:28 (Cyclingnews: 'promising fourth, 28 seconds back'). Del Toro gap confirmed +0:51 per Cyclingnews. Ayuso confirmed +1:16, finishing 38th.
